attempt tracking configuration.\n * Tracks failed reset attempts to prevent brute force attacks.\n */\nconst RESET_ATTEMPT_WINDOW_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000; // 15 minutes\nconst MAX_RESET_ATTEMPTS = 5; // Maximum attempts before lockout\nconst ACCOUNT_LOCKOUT_DURATION_MS = 30 * 60 * 1000; // 30 minutes\n\n/**\n * Token cleanup interval.\n * Runs periodic cleanup of expired tokens.\n */\nconst TOKEN_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes\n\n// ============================================================================\n// In-Memory Storage (Replace with database in production)\n// ============================================================================\n\nconst passwordResetTokens = new Map<string, PasswordResetToken>();\nconst passwordHistory = new Map<string, PasswordHistoryEntry[]>();\nconst passwordValidationAttempts = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();\n\n/**\n * Password reset attempt tracking.\n * Tracks failed reset attempts per email/IP combination to prevent brute force.\n * Key format: \"email:ip\" or \"email\" for IP-agnostic tracking\n */\nconst passwordResetAttempts = new Map<\n  string,\n  { count: number; resetAt: number; lockedUntil?: number }\n>();\n\n/**\n * Account lockout tracking.\n * Tracks accounts that are temporarily locked due to suspicious activity.\n */\nconst accountLockouts = new Map<\n  string,\n  { lockedUntil: number; reason: string; attempts: number }\n>();\n\n/**\n * Token cleanup interval reference.\n */\nlet tokenCleanupInterval: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;\n\n/**\n * Breached password cache to reduce API calls.\n * Stores password hash prefixes that have been checked.\n */\nconst breachedPasswordCache = new Map<\n  string,\n  { breached: boolean; count: number; expiresAt: number }\n>();\n\n/**\n * Cache TTL for breached password results (1 hour).\n * Passwords that change breach status frequently should be re-checked.\n */\nconst BREACHED_PASSWORD_CACHE_TTL_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000;\n\n/**\n * Pepper for additional password security.\n * In production, this should be stored securely (e.g., in a secrets manager)\n * and loaded via environment variable or secure configuration.\n *\n * The pepper is a server-wide secret that is combined with the password\n * before hashing. This provides defense in depth even if the database\n * is compromised, as the attacker would also need the pepper.\n *\n * @see https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Password_Storage_Cheat_Sheet.html\n */\nlet PASSWORD_PEPPER = \"\";\n\n/**\n * Set the password pepper for additional security.\n *\n * IMPORTANT: In production, this should be loaded from a secure source\n * like a secrets manager or environment variable. Never hardcode the pepper.\n *\n * @param pepper - The pepper value to use (hex encoded string), or empty string to disable\n */\nexport function setPasswordPepper(pepper: string): void {\n  // Allow empty string to disable pepper\n  if (pepper === \"\") {\n    PASSWORD_PEPPER = \"\";\n    logger.info(\"Password pepper disabled\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Validate pepper is a valid hex string\n  if (!/^[a-fA-F0-9]+$/.test(pepper)) {\n    throw new Error(\"Password pepper must be a hex string\");\n  }\n  if (pepper.length < 32) {\n    throw new Error(\"Password pepper must be at least 16 bytes (32 hex chars)\");\n  }\n  PASSWORD_PEPPER = pepper;\n  logger.info(\"Password pepper configured\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the current password pepper.\n * Returns an empty string if not configured.\n *\n * @internal\n */\nfunction getPasswordPepper(): string {\n  return PASSWORD_PEPPER;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Password Reset Attempt Tracking & Account Lockout\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Check if an account is currently locked due to too many failed reset attempts.\n *\n * @param email - The email address to check\n * @returns Object indicating if locked and when it will be unlocked\n */\nexport function isAccountLocked(email: string): {\n  locked: boolean;\n  reason?: string;\n  unlockTime?: number;\n  remainingMinutes?: number;\n} {\n  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n  const lockout = accountLockouts.get(normalizedEmail);\n\n  if (!lockout) {\n    return { locked: false };\n  }\n\n  // Check if lockout has expired\n  if (Date.now() > lockout.lockedUntil) {\n    accountLockouts.delete(normalizedEmail);\n    passwordResetAttempts.delete(normalizedEmail);\n    return { locked: false };\n  }\n\n  const remainingMinutes = Math.ceil((lockout.lockedUntil - Date.now()) / (60 * 1000));\n\n  return {\n    locked: true,\n    reason: lockout.reason,\n    unlockTime: lockout.lockedUntil,\n    remainingMinutes,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Record a failed password reset attempt.\n * Tracks attempts and implements account lockout after too many failures.\n *\n * @param email - The email address\n * @param clientIp - The client IP address\n * @returns Object indicating if account is now locked and details\n */\nexport function recordFailedResetAttempt(\n  email: string,\n  clientIp: string\n): {\n  locked: boolean;\n  attemptCount: number;\n  remainingAttempts: number;\n  lockedUntil?: number;\n} {\n  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n  const key = `${normalizedEmail}:${clientIp}`;\n  const now = Date.now();\n\n  let attempt = passwordResetAttempts.get(key);\n\n  // Reset if window has expired\n  if (!attempt || now > attempt.resetAt) {\n    attempt = { count: 0, resetAt: now + RESET_ATTEMPT_WINDOW_MS };\n  }\n\n  attempt.count++;\n  passwordResetAttempts.set(key, attempt);\n\n  // Also track IP-agnostic attempts (same email from any IP)\n  let globalAttempt = passwordResetAttempts.get(normalizedEmail);\n  if (!globalAttempt || now > globalAttempt.resetAt) {\n    globalAttempt = { count: 0, resetAt: now + RESET_ATTEMPT_WINDOW_MS };\n  }\n  globalAttempt.count++;\n  passwordResetAttempts.set(normalizedEmail, globalAttempt);\n\n  const remainingAttempts = Math.max(0, MAX_RESET_ATTEMPTS - globalAttempt.count);\n\n  // Check if we should lock the account\n  if (globalAttempt.count >= MAX_RESET_ATTEMPTS) {\n    const lockedUntil = now + ACCOUNT_LOCKOUT_DURATION_MS;\n    accountLockouts.set(normalizedEmail, {\n      lockedUntil,\n      reason: \"Too many failed password reset attempts\",\n      attempts: globalAttempt.count,\n    });\n\n    logger.warn(\"Account locked due to too many failed reset attempts\", {\n      email: normalizedEmail,\n      clientIp,\n      attempts: globalAttempt.count,\n      lockedUntil: new Date(lockedUntil).toISOString(),\n    });\n\n    return {\n      locked: true,\n      attemptCount: globalAttempt.count,\n      remainingAttempts: 0,\n      lockedUntil,\n    };\n  }\n\n  return {\n    locked: false,\n    attemptCount: globalAttempt.count,\n    remainingAttempts,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Clear failed reset attempts for an email (e.g., after successful reset).\n *\n * @param email - The email address\n */\nexport function clearFailedResetAttempts(email: string): void {\n  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n\n  // Remove all entries that start with this email\n  for (const key of passwordResetAttempts.keys()) {\n    if (key === normalizedEmail || key.startsWith(`${normalizedEmail}:`)) {\n      passwordResetAttempts.delete(key);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Also remove from lockouts\n  accountLockouts.delete(normalizedEmail);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get current failed reset attempt count for an email/IP combination.\n *\n * @param email - The email address\n * @param clientIp - The client IP address\n * @returns Number of failed attempts\n */\nexport function getFailedResetAttemptCount(email: string, clientIp: string): number {\n  const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase();\n  const key = `${normalizedEmail}:${clientIp}`;\n  const attempt = passwordResetAttempts.get(key);\n\n  if (!attempt || Date.now() > attempt.resetAt) {\n    return 0;\n  }\n\n  return attempt.count;\n}\n\n/**\n * Start the automatic token cleanup interval.\n * Removes expired tokens periodically to prevent memory leaks.\n *\n * @internal\n */\nexport function _startTokenCleanup(): void {\n  if (tokenCleanupInterval) {\n    return; // Already running\n  }\n\n  tokenCleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {\n    const now = Date.now();\n    let cleaned = 0;\n\n    for (const [tokenId, tokenData] of passwordResetTokens.entries()) {\n      // Remove expired or used tokens\n      if (now > tokenData.expiresAt || tokenData.used) {\n        passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n        cleaned++;\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Clean expired lockouts\n    for (const [email, lockout] of accountLockouts.entries()) {\n      if (now > lockout.lockedUntil) {\n        accountLockouts.delete(email);\n        passwordResetAttempts.delete(email);\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (cleaned > 0) {\n      logger.debug(\"Token cleanup completed\", { cleaned, remaining: passwordResetTokens.size });\n    }\n  }, TOKEN_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS);\n\n  logger.info(\"Token cleanup interval started\", { intervalMs: TOKEN_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS });\n}\n\n/**\n * Stop the automatic token cleanup interval.\n *\n * @internal\n */\nexport function _stopTokenCleanup(): void {\n  if (tokenCleanupInterval) {\n    clearInterval(tokenCleanupInterval);\n    tokenCleanupInterval = null;\n    logger.info(\"Token cleanup interval stopped\");\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Manually trigger token cleanup.\n * Useful for testing or immediate cleanup.\n *\n * @returns Number of tokens cleaned up\n */\nexport function cleanupExpiredTokens(): number {\n  const now = Date.now();\n  let cleaned = 0;\n\n  for (const [tokenId, tokenData] of passwordResetTokens.entries()) {\n    if (now > tokenData.expiresAt || tokenData.used) {\n      passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n      cleaned++;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Clean expired lockouts\n  for (const [email, lockout] of accountLockouts.entries()) {\n    if (now > lockout.lockedUntil) {\n      accountLockouts.delete(email);\n      passwordResetAttempts.delete(email);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return cleaned;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Password Validation\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Detect sequential character patterns in password.\n * Checks for:\n * - Numeric sequences: 1234, 4321, 0123\n * - Alphabetic sequences: abcd, DCBA, mnop\n * - Both forward and reverse sequences\n *\n * @param password - The password to check\n * @param maxSequence - Maximum allowed sequential characters\n * @returns Object indicating if sequential pattern was found and the pattern\n */\nfunction detectSequentialChars(\n  password: string,\n  maxSequence: number\n): { found: boolean; pattern?: string } {\n  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n\n  // Check each position for sequences\n  for (let i = 0; i < lowerPassword.length - (maxSequence - 1); i++) {\n    // Extract potential sequence\n    const sequence = lowerPassword.substring(i, i + maxSequence + 1);\n\n    // Check if it's a valid sequence (all letters or all digits)\n    const isAllLetters = /^[a-z]+$/.test(sequence);\n    const isAllDigits = /^\\d+$/.test(sequence);\n\n    if (!isAllLetters && !isAllDigits) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    // Check for forward or reverse sequential pattern\n    if (isSequentialSequence(sequence)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: sequence };\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { found: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a string is a sequential character pattern.\n * Handles both forward (abcd, 1234) and reverse (dcba, 4321) sequences.\n */\nfunction isSequentialSequence(str: string): boolean {\n  if (str.length < 2) return false;\n\n  // Check forward sequence\n  let isForward = true;\n  let isReverse = true;\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < str.length - 1; i++) {\n    const current = str.charCodeAt(i);\n    const next = str.charCodeAt(i + 1);\n\n    // Forward: next char is exactly +1\n    if (next !== current + 1) {\n      isForward = false;\n    }\n\n    // Reverse: next char is exactly -1\n    if (next !== current - 1) {\n      isReverse = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return isForward || isReverse;\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect keyboard walking patterns in password.\n * Checks for common keyboard sequences like \"qwerty\", \"asdf\", \"1qaz\", etc.\n *\n * Only reports patterns of 3+ characters to avoid false positives on\n * single characters that happen to be part of keyboard layouts.\n *\n * @param password - The password to check\n * @returns Object indicating if keyboard pattern was found and the pattern\n */\nfunction detectKeyboardPatterns(password: string): { found: boolean; pattern?: string } {\n  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n  const MIN_PATTERN_LENGTH = 3;\n\n  // Check each pattern\n  for (const pattern of KEYBOARD_PATTERNS) {\n    // Skip patterns shorter than minimum length\n    if (pattern.length < MIN_PATTERN_LENGTH) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    // Check if pattern exists in password (case-insensitive)\n    if (lowerPassword.includes(pattern)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern };\n    }\n\n    // Also check reversed version of pattern\n    const reversed = pattern.split(\"\").reverse().join(\"\");\n    if (lowerPassword.includes(reversed)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: reversed };\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { found: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect common password variations like \"password1\", \"password2\", etc.\n *\n * @param password - The password to check\n * @param skipKeyboardBased - Whether to skip keyboard-based common words (when blockKeyboardPatterns is false)\n * @returns Object indicating if common variation was found\n */\nfunction detectCommonVariations(\n  password: string,\n  skipKeyboardBased = false\n): { found: boolean; pattern?: string } {\n  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n\n  // Common base passwords with number/special char variations\n  // Keyboard-based words (qwerty, asdf) are skipped when blockKeyboardPatterns is false\n  const commonBases = [\n    \"password\",\n    \"passw0rd\",\n    \"admin\",\n    \"welcome\",\n    \"login\",\n    // Skip keyboard-based words when requested\n    ...(skipKeyboardBased ? [] : [\"qwerty\"]),\n    \"letmein\",\n    \"monkey\",\n    \"dragon\",\n    \"master\",\n  ];\n\n  for (const base of commonBases) {\n    // Check if password starts with common base\n    if (!lowerPassword.startsWith(base)) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    const suffix = lowerPassword.slice(base.length);\n\n    // If there's no suffix, it's just the base word (handled elsewhere)\n    if (suffix.length === 0) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    // Check if suffix is only numbers\n    if (/^\\d+$/.test(suffix)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[numbers]\" };\n    }\n\n    // Check if suffix is only special characters\n    if (/^[!@#$%^&*]+$/.test(suffix)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[special]\" };\n    }\n\n    // Check if suffix is a year (19xx or 20xx)\n    if (/^(19\\d\\d|20\\d\\d)$/.test(suffix)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[year]\" };\n    }\n\n    // Check if suffix is numbers followed by special chars (e.g., \"password123!@#\")\n    if (/^\\d+[!@#$%^&*]+$/.test(suffix)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[numbers+special]\" };\n    }\n\n    // Check if suffix is special chars followed by numbers (e.g., \"password!@#123\")\n    if (/^[!@#$%^&*]+\\d+$/.test(suffix)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[special+numbers]\" };\n    }\n\n    // Check if suffix is a year followed by special chars (e.g., \"password2024!@#\")\n    if (/^(19\\d\\d|20\\d\\d)[!@#$%^&*]+$/.test(suffix)) {\n      return { found: true, pattern: base + \"[year+special]\" };\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { found: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate a password against policy requirements.\n *\n * This function performs comprehensive password validation including:\n * - Length requirements\n * - Character complexity\n * - Common password detection\n * - Character repetition\n * - Breached password checking (via HIBP k-anonymity API)\n * - Password strength scoring\n *\n * @param password - The password to validate\n * @param policy - Optional custom policy (uses defaults if not provided)\n * @param keyId - Optional key ID for password history checking\n * @returns Validation result with detailed feedback\n */\nexport async function validatePassword(\n  password: string,\n  policy: PasswordPolicy = {},\n  keyId?: string\n): Promise<PasswordValidationResult> {\n  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n\n  // Check maximum length FIRST before any sanitization\n  // This ensures we catch long passwords and provide a clear error message\n  if (password.length > mergedPolicy.maxLength) {\n    errors.push(`Password must be no more than ${mergedPolicy.maxLength} characters long`);\n    return {\n      valid: false,\n      errors,\n      strength: 0,\n      strengthCategory: \"weak\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Check minimum length\n  if (password.length < mergedPolicy.minLength) {\n    errors.push(`Password must be at least ${mergedPolicy.minLength} characters long`);\n  }\n\n  // Sanitize input to prevent injection attacks (without length truncation since we already checked)\n  // For passwords, we only do basic sanitization - no SQL/Command injection prevention\n  // since those would incorrectly reject valid passwords containing words like \"or\", \"and\", etc.\n  const sanitizedPassword = sanitizeStringSimple(password, {\n    maxLength: password.length, // Don't truncate, we already validated length\n    preserveCase: true,\n    preserveWhitespace: mergedPolicy.allowSpaces,\n    preventSqlInjection: false, // Don't prevent SQL injection in passwords\n    preventCommandInjection: false, // Don't prevent command injection in passwords\n    preventLdapInjection: false, // Don't prevent LDAP injection in passwords\n    preventNosqlInjection: false, // Don't prevent NoSQL injection in passwords\n  });\n\n  // Check if sanitization changed the password (injection patterns detected)\n  // We only check for XSS patterns in passwords since those are actual security risks\n  if (sanitizedPassword !== password) {\n    errors.push(\"Password contains invalid characters\");\n    return {\n      valid: false,\n      errors,\n      strength: 0,\n      strengthCategory: \"weak\",\n    };\n  }\n\n  // Check uppercase\n  if (mergedPolicy.requireUppercase && !/[A-Z]/.test(password)) {\n    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one uppercase letter\");\n  }\n\n  // Check lowercase\n  if (mergedPolicy.requireLowercase && !/[a-z]/.test(password)) {\n    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one lowercase letter\");\n  }\n\n  // Check numbers\n  if (mergedPolicy.requireNumbers && !/\\d/.test(password)) {\n    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one number\");\n  }\n\n  // Check special characters\n  if (mergedPolicy.requireSpecialChars && !/[!@#$%^&*()_+\\-=[\\]{};':\"\\\\|,.<>/?]/.test(password)) {\n    errors.push(\"Password must contain at least one special character\");\n  }\n\n  // Check spaces\n  if (!mergedPolicy.allowSpaces && /\\s/.test(password)) {\n    errors.push(\"Password cannot contain spaces\");\n  }\n\n  // Check against blocked passwords (exact match only)\n  // Substring matching is too aggressive and would reject valid passwords\n  // like \"SecurePass123!\" that happen to contain common words\n  const lowerPassword = password.toLowerCase();\n  for (const blocked of mergedPolicy.blockedPasswords) {\n    if (lowerPassword === blocked) {\n      errors.push(\"Password is too common or weak\");\n      break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check character repetition\n  if (mergedPolicy.maxRepetition > 0) {\n    const repetitionRegex = new RegExp(`(.)\\\\1{${mergedPolicy.maxRepetition},}`, \"g\");\n    if (repetitionRegex.test(password)) {\n      errors.push(\n        `Password cannot contain the same character more than ${mergedPolicy.maxRepetition} times in a row`\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check sequential character patterns\n  if (mergedPolicy.blockSequentialChars && mergedPolicy.maxSequentialChars > 0) {\n    const sequentialResult = detectSequentialChars(password, mergedPolicy.maxSequentialChars);\n    if (sequentialResult.found) {\n      errors.push(\n        `Password contains sequential characters (${sequentialResult.pattern}). Avoid using patterns like \"1234\" or \"abcd\".`\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check keyboard walking patterns\n  if (mergedPolicy.blockKeyboardPatterns) {\n    const keyboardResult = detectKeyboardPatterns(password);\n    if (keyboardResult.found) {\n      errors.push(\n        `Password contains keyboard pattern (${keyboardResult.pattern}). Avoid using keyboard sequences.`\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check common password variations\n  // Skip keyboard-based words when blockKeyboardPatterns is disabled\n  const variationResult = detectCommonVariations(password, !mergedPolicy.blockKeyboardPatterns);\n  if (variationResult.found) {\n    errors.push(\n      `Password contains a common variation pattern (${variationResult.pattern}). Please choose a more unique password.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Calculate strength score\n  const strength = calculatePasswordStrength(password);\n  const strengthCategory = getStrengthCategory(strength);\n\n  // Check password history if keyId provided\n  if (keyId && mergedPolicy.passwordHistoryCount > 0) {\n    const history = passwordHistory.get(keyId);\n    if (history) {\n      // Check against recent passwords\n      const recentHistory = history.slice(-mergedPolicy.passwordHistoryCount);\n      for (const entry of recentHistory) {\n        if (await verifyPasswordHash(password, entry.hash, entry.salt)) {\n          errors.push(\"Cannot reuse a recent password\");\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check breached passwords via HIBP API\n  let breached = false;\n  let breachCount = 0;\n  if (mergedPolicy.checkBreachedPasswords && errors.length === 0) {\n    const breachResult = await checkBreachedPassword(password);\n    breached = breachResult.breached;\n    breachCount = breachResult.count;\n\n    if (breached && breachCount > (mergedPolicy.maxBreachedCount ?? 0)) {\n      errors.push(\n        `This password has been found in data breaches ${breachCount} times. Please choose a different password.`\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check minimum strength score\n  if (strength < (mergedPolicy.minStrengthScore ?? 0)) {\n    errors.push(\n      `Password is too weak (strength: ${strength}/100). Please choose a stronger password.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  return {\n    valid: errors.length === 0,\n    errors,\n    strength,\n    strengthCategory,\n    breached,\n    breachCount: breached ? breachCount : undefined,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate password strength score (0-100).\n *\n * Uses entropy-based calculation with bonuses for:\n * - Length\n * - Character variety\n * - Character distribution\n */\nfunction calculatePasswordStrength(password: string): number {\n  let score = 0;\n\n  // Length score (up to 40 points)\n  score += Math.min(40, password.length * 2);\n\n  // Character variety (up to 30 points)\n  if (/[a-z]/.test(password)) score += 5;\n  if (/[A-Z]/.test(password)) score += 5;\n  if (/\\d/.test(password)) score += 5;\n  if (/[!@#$%^&*()_+\\-=[\\]{};':\"\\\\|,.<>/?]/.test(password)) score += 10;\n  if (/\\s/.test(password)) score += 5;\n\n  // Character distribution (up to 20 points)\n  const uniqueChars = new Set(password).size;\n  score += Math.min(20, (uniqueChars / password.length) * 20);\n\n  // Complexity bonus (up to 10 points)\n  const hasMixedCase = /[a-z]/.test(password) && /[A-Z]/.test(password);\n  const hasNumbers = /\\d/.test(password);\n\n  // eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-escape\n  const hasSpecial = /[!@#$%^&*()_+\\-=\\[\\]{};':\"\\\\|,.<>\\/?]/.test(password);\n  if (hasMixedCase && hasNumbers && hasSpecial) score += 10;\n\n  return Math.min(100, score);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get strength category from score.\n */\nfunction getStrengthCategory(score: number): \"weak\" | \"fair\" | \"good\" | \"strong\" {\n  if (score < 40) return \"weak\";\n  if (score < 60) return \"fair\";\n  if (score < 80) return \"good\";\n  return \"strong\";\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a password has been breached using Have I Been Pwned k-anonymity API.\n *\n * Uses the k-anonymity model where only the first 5 characters of the\n * SHA-256 hash are sent to the API. The full hash never leaves the server.\n *\n * Results are cached for 1 hour to reduce API calls and improve performance.\n *\n * @param password - The password to check\n * @returns Object indicating if breached and the count\n */\nasync function checkBreachedPassword(\n  password: string\n): Promise<{ breached: boolean; count: number }> {\n  try {\n    // Calculate SHA-256 hash of password\n    const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n    const data = encoder.encode(password);\n    const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest(\"SHA-256\", data);\n    const hashArray = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer));\n    const hash = hashArray.map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\")).join(\"\");\n\n    // Get first 5 characters for API request (cache key)\n    const prefix = hash.substring(0, 5);\n    const suffix = hash.substring(5);\n\n    // Check cache first\n    const cached = breachedPasswordCache.get(prefix);\n    const now = Date.now();\n    if (cached && now < cached.expiresAt) {\n      // Check if our specific suffix matches\n      if (cached.breached) {\n        return { breached: true, count: cached.count };\n      }\n      // If cached as not breached, we still need to check if our suffix is in the list\n      // because the prefix can have multiple suffixes with different breach counts\n    }\n\n    // Query HIBP API\n    const response = await fetch(`https://api.pwnedpasswords.com/range/${prefix}`, {\n      method: \"GET\",\n      headers: {\n        \"User-Agent\": \"MTA-My-Way-Server/1.0\",\n      },\n    });\n\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      logger.warn(\"Failed to check breached password\", { status: response.status });\n      return { breached: false, count: 0 };\n    }\n\n    const body = await response.text();\n    const lines = body.split(\"\\n\");\n\n    // Check if our suffix is in the results\n    for (const line of lines) {\n      const [lineSuffix, countStr] = line.split(\":\");\n      if (lineSuffix?.toUpperCase() === suffix.toUpperCase()) {\n        const count = parseInt(countStr ?? \"0\", 10);\n\n        // Cache the result\n        breachedPasswordCache.set(prefix, {\n          breached: true,\n          count,\n          expiresAt: now + BREACHED_PASSWORD_CACHE_TTL_MS,\n        });\n\n        return { breached: true, count };\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Cache the not-breached result\n    breachedPasswordCache.set(prefix, {\n      breached: false,\n      count: 0,\n      expiresAt: now + BREACHED_PASSWORD_CACHE_TTL_MS,\n    });\n\n    return { breached: false, count: 0 };\n  } catch (error) {\n    logger.warn(\"Error checking breached password\", error as Error);\n    return { breached: false, count: 0 };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Clear the breached password cache.\n *\n * This can be useful for testing or if you want to force fresh checks.\n */\nexport function clearBreachedPasswordCache(): void {\n  breachedPasswordCache.clear();\n  logger.info(\"Breached password cache cleared\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Password Hashing\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Hash a password using Argon2id.\n *\n * Argon2id is the winner of the Password Hashing Competition (2015)\n * and is recommended by OWASP for new applications.\n *\n * Uses OWASP 2024 recommended parameters:\n * - Memory: 64 MiB\n * - Iterations: 3\n * - Parallelism: 4\n * - Salt length: 32 bytes\n * - Output length: 32 bytes\n *\n * @param password - The password to hash\n * @returns Promise containing the hash data\n */\nexport async function hashPassword(password: string): Promise<PasswordHash> {\n  // Validate password is not empty\n  if (!password || password.length === 0) {\n    throw new Error(\"Password cannot be empty\");\n  }\n\n  // Combine password with pepper for additional security (if configured)\n  const pepper = getPasswordPepper();\n  const passwordWithPepper = pepper ? password + pepper : password;\n\n  // Hash using Argon2id with OWASP 2024 recommended parameters\n  // The argon2 package handles salt generation automatically\n  const hash = await argon2.hash(passwordWithPepper, ARGON2_OPTIONS);\n\n  // Extract the salt from the encoded hash\n  // Argon2 format: $argon2id$v=19$m=65536,t=3,p=4$salt$hash\n  const parts = hash.split(\"$\");\n  const salt = parts[4]; // Extract salt from encoded hash\n\n  return {\n    hash,\n    salt,\n    algorithm: \"argon2id\",\n    iterations: ARGON2_OPTIONS.timeCost ?? 3,\n    memoryCost: ARGON2_OPTIONS.memoryCost,\n    parallelism: ARGON2_OPTIONS.parallelism,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Verify a password against a stored Argon2id hash.\n *\n * Uses the argon2 package's built-in verify function which handles\n * timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks.\n *\n * @param password - The password to verify\n * @param hash - The stored Argon2id hash (encoded string)\n * @param salt - The salt used for hashing (not needed for Argon2, kept for API compatibility)\n * @returns Promise indicating if the password matches\n */\nexport async function verifyPasswordHash(\n  password: string,\n  hash: string,\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  salt: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  // Validate inputs\n  if (!password || password.length === 0) {\n    return false;\n  }\n  if (!hash) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    // Combine password with pepper for verification (must match hashing)\n    const pepper = getPasswordPepper();\n    const passwordWithPepper = pepper ? password + pepper : password;\n\n    // Use argon2.verify which handles timing-safe comparison\n    return await argon2.verify(hash, passwordWithPepper);\n  } catch (error) {\n    // If verification fails (invalid hash format, etc.), return false\n    logger.warn(\"Password verification failed\", { error: error as Error });\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Store a password in history for a key.\n *\n * @param keyId - The key ID\n * @param hash - The password hash\n * @param salt - The salt used\n */\nexport function storePasswordInHistory(keyId: string, hash: string, salt: string): void {\n  const history = passwordHistory.get(keyId) ?? [];\n  const entry: PasswordHistoryEntry = {\n    hash,\n    salt,\n    timestamp: Date.now(),\n  };\n  history.push(entry);\n\n  // Keep only the last N passwords\n  if (history.length > DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY.passwordHistoryCount) {\n    history.shift();\n  }\n\n  passwordHistory.set(keyId, history);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get password history for a key.\n *\n * @param keyId - The key ID\n * @returns Array of password history entries\n */\nexport function getPasswordHistory(keyId: string): PasswordHistoryEntry[] {\n  return passwordHistory.get(keyId) ?? [];\n}\n\n/**\n * Clear password history for a key.\n *\n * @param keyId - The key ID\n * @returns Number of entries cleared\n */\nexport function clearPasswordHistory(keyId: string): number {\n  const history = passwordHistory.get(keyId);\n  if (!history) return 0;\n  const count = history.length;\n  passwordHistory.delete(keyId);\n  return count;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Password Reset Flow\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Rate limit password validation attempts to prevent enumeration attacks.\n * @private Reserved for future use\n */\nfunction _checkPasswordValidationRateLimit(clientIp: string): boolean {\n  const now = Date.now();\n  const record = passwordValidationAttempts.get(clientIp);\n\n  if (!record || now > record.resetAt) {\n    passwordValidationAttempts.set(clientIp, {\n      count: 1,\n      resetAt: now + PASSWORD_VALIDATION_WINDOW_MS,\n    });\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  if (record.count >= MAX_PASSWORD_VALIDATIONS_PER_MINUTE) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  record.count++;\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate a device fingerprint from user agent for token verification.\n */\nasync function generateDeviceFingerprint(userAgent?: string): Promise<string> {\n  if (!userAgent) return \"unknown\";\n  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n  const data = encoder.encode(userAgent);\n  const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest(\"SHA-256\", data);\n  const hashArray = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer));\n  return hashArray\n    .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\"))\n    .join(\"\")\n    .substring(0, 16);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get user-friendly device information from user agent.\n */\nexport function getDeviceInfo(userAgent?: string): {\n  deviceType: string;\n  browser: string;\n  os: string;\n} {\n  if (!userAgent) {\n    return { deviceType: \"Unknown\", browser: \"Unknown\", os: \"Unknown\" };\n  }\n\n  const ua = userAgent.toLowerCase();\n\n  // Detect device type\n  let deviceType = \"desktop\";\n  if (/mobile|android|iphone|ipod|blackberry|iemobile|opera mini/i.test(ua)) {\n    deviceType = \"mobile\";\n  } else if (/tablet|ipad|playbook|silk/i.test(ua)) {\n    deviceType = \"tablet\";\n  }\n\n  // Detect browser\n  let browser = \"Unknown\";\n  if (/chrome|crios/i.test(ua) && !/edge|opr|brave/i.test(ua)) {\n    browser = \"Chrome\";\n  } else if (/safari/i.test(ua) && !/chrome/i.test(ua)) {\n    browser = \"Safari\";\n  } else if (/firefox/i.test(ua)) {\n    browser = \"Firefox\";\n  } else if (/edge|edg/i.test(ua)) {\n    browser = \"Edge\";\n  } else if (/opr|opera/i.test(ua)) {\n    browser = \"Opera\";\n  } else if (/brave/i.test(ua)) {\n    browser = \"Brave\";\n  }\n\n  // Detect OS - check mobile OS first before desktop\n  let os = \"Unknown\";\n  if (/android/i.test(ua)) {\n    os = \"Android\";\n  } else if (/ios|iphone|ipad|ipod/i.test(ua)) {\n    os = \"iOS\";\n  } else if (/windows/i.test(ua)) {\n    os = \"Windows\";\n  } else if (/macintosh|mac os x/i.test(ua)) {\n    os = \"macOS\";\n  } else if (/linux/i.test(ua)) {\n    os = \"Linux\";\n  }\n\n  return { deviceType, browser, os };\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate a secure password reset token.\n *\n * @param keyId - The key ID for the user\n * @param clientIp - The client IP address\n * @param userAgent - Optional user agent string for device verification\n * @returns Object containing the raw token (for sending to user) and token data\n */\nexport async function generatePasswordResetToken(\n  keyId: string,\n  clientIp: string,\n  userAgent?: string\n): Promise<{\n  token: string;\n  tokenId: string;\n  expiresAt: number;\n  deviceInfo?: ReturnType<typeof getDeviceInfo>;\n}> {\n  // Generate random token\n  const tokenBytes = new Uint8Array(RESET_TOKEN_LENGTH);\n  crypto.getRandomValues(tokenBytes);\n  const token = Array.from(tokenBytes, (b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\")).join(\"\");\n\n  // Generate token ID\n  const tokenId = crypto.randomUUID();\n\n  // Calculate SHA-256 hash of token (for storage)\n  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n  const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest(\"SHA-256\", encoder.encode(token));\n  const hashArray = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer));\n  const tokenHash = hashArray.map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\")).join(\"\");\n\n  // Generate device fingerprint for verification\n  const deviceFingerprint = await generateDeviceFingerprint(userAgent);\n\n  // Create token data\n  const tokenData: PasswordResetToken = {\n    tokenId,\n    keyId,\n    tokenHash,\n    createdAt: Date.now(),\n    expiresAt: Date.now() + RESET_TOKEN_TTL_MS,\n    used: false,\n    clientIp,\n    userAgent,\n    deviceFingerprint,\n  };\n\n  // Store token\n  passwordResetTokens.set(tokenId, tokenData);\n\n  const deviceInfo = getDeviceInfo(userAgent);\n\n  logger.info(\"Password reset token generated\", {\n    tokenId,\n    keyId,\n    clientIp,\n    deviceInfo,\n  });\n\n  return {\n    token, // Return raw token for sending to user (e.g., email)\n    tokenId,\n    expiresAt: tokenData.expiresAt,\n    deviceInfo,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate a password reset token.\n *\n * @param tokenId - The token ID\n * @param token - The raw token from the email/link\n * @param clientIp - The client IP address\n * @param userAgent - Optional user agent for device verification\n * @returns Object with keyId if valid, and a warning if device changed\n */\nexport async function validatePasswordResetToken(\n  tokenId: string,\n  token: string,\n  clientIp: string,\n  userAgent?: string\n): Promise<{ keyId: string | null; deviceChanged: boolean; warning?: string }> {\n  const tokenData = passwordResetTokens.get(tokenId);\n\n  if (!tokenData) {\n    logger.warn(\"Invalid password reset token ID\", { tokenId, clientIp });\n    return { keyId: null, deviceChanged: false };\n  }\n\n  // Check expiration\n  if (Date.now() > tokenData.expiresAt) {\n    passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n    logger.warn(\"Expired password reset token\", { tokenId, clientIp });\n    return { keyId: null, deviceChanged: false };\n  }\n\n  // Check if already used\n  if (tokenData.used) {\n    passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n    logger.warn(\"Reused password reset token\", { tokenId, clientIp, keyId: tokenData.keyId });\n    return { keyId: null, deviceChanged: false };\n  }\n\n  // Verify token hash\n  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n  const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest(\"SHA-256\", encoder.encode(token));\n  const hashArray = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer));\n  const tokenHash = hashArray.map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\")).join(\"\");\n\n  if (tokenHash !== tokenData.tokenHash) {\n    logger.warn(\"Invalid password reset token\", { tokenId, clientIp });\n    return { keyId: null, deviceChanged: false };\n  }\n\n  // Check device fingerprint if userAgent provided\n  let deviceChanged = false;\n  let warning: string | undefined;\n\n  if (userAgent && tokenData.deviceFingerprint) {\n    const currentFingerprint = await generateDeviceFingerprint(userAgent);\n    if (currentFingerprint !== tokenData.deviceFingerprint) {\n      deviceChanged = true;\n      const originalDevice = getDeviceInfo(tokenData.userAgent);\n      const currentDevice = getDeviceInfo(userAgent);\n      warning = `This reset link was requested from a different device (${originalDevice.deviceType}/${originalDevice.browser}). For your security, please verify you requested this password reset.`;\n      logger.warn(\"Password reset device mismatch\", {\n        tokenId,\n        originalDevice,\n        currentDevice,\n      });\n      // Still allow the reset but log for monitoring\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Verify client IP matches (optional security measure - logs mismatch but allows)\n  if (tokenData.clientIp !== clientIp) {\n    logger.warn(\"Password reset token IP mismatch\", {\n      tokenId,\n      expectedIp: tokenData.clientIp,\n      receivedIp: clientIp,\n    });\n    // Still allow but log for monitoring\n  }\n\n  return { keyId: tokenData.keyId, deviceChanged, warning };\n}\n\n/**\n * Consume a password reset token (mark as used).\n *\n * @param tokenId - The token ID\n * @returns True if successfully consumed\n */\nexport function consumePasswordResetToken(tokenId: string): boolean {\n  const tokenData = passwordResetTokens.get(tokenId);\n\n  if (!tokenData) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Mark as used\n  tokenData.used = true;\n\n  // Delete after a short delay (for logging purposes)\n  setTimeout(() => {\n    passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n  }, 5000);\n\n  logger.info(\"Password reset token consumed\", { tokenId, keyId: tokenData.keyId });\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Invalidate all existing reset tokens for a key ID.\n *\n * @param keyId - The key ID\n * @returns Number of tokens invalidated\n */\nexport function invalidateResetTokensForKey(keyId: string): number {\n  let count = 0;\n  for (const [tokenId, tokenData] of passwordResetTokens.entries()) {\n    if (tokenData.keyId === keyId && !tokenData.used) {\n      passwordResetTokens.delete(tokenId);\n      count++;\n    }\n  }\n  return count;\n}\n\n/**\n * Set a token's expiration time for testing */\nexport function _getPasswordResetTokensMap(): Map<string, PasswordResetToken> {\n  return passwordResetTokens;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Password Expiration and Rotation\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Check if a password has expired based on policy.\n *\n * @param passwordSetAt - Timestamp when password was set\n * @param policy - Optional custom policy\n * @returns True if password has expired\n */\nexport function isPasswordExpired(passwordSetAt: number, policy: PasswordPolicy = {}): boolean {\n  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n  const expirationDays = mergedPolicy.passwordExpirationDays ?? 0;\n\n  if (expirationDays === 0) {\n    return false; // No expiration\n  }\n\n  const expirationMs = expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n  return Date.now() > passwordSetAt + expirationMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a password is in the grace period (expired but can still be used).\n *\n * @param passwordSetAt - Timestamp when password was set\n * @param policy - Optional custom policy\n * @returns True if password is in grace period\n */\nexport function isPasswordInGracePeriod(\n  passwordSetAt: number,\n  policy: PasswordPolicy = {}\n): boolean {\n  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n  const expirationDays = mergedPolicy.passwordExpirationDays ?? 0;\n  const gracePeriodDays = mergedPolicy.passwordGracePeriodDays ?? 0;\n\n  if (expirationDays === 0) {\n    return false; // No expiration means no grace period\n  }\n\n  if (gracePeriodDays === 0) {\n    return false; // No grace period configured\n  }\n\n  const expirationMs = expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n  const gracePeriodMs = gracePeriodDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n  const now = Date.now();\n  const expiresAt = passwordSetAt + expirationMs;\n  const graceEndsAt = expiresAt + gracePeriodMs;\n\n  // In grace period if expired but still within grace window\n  return now > expiresAt && now <= graceEndsAt;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the number of days remaining in the grace period.\n *\n * @param passwordSetAt - Timestamp when password was set\n * @param policy - Optional custom policy\n * @returns Days remaining in grace period, or null if not in grace period\n */\nexport function getDaysInGracePeriod(\n  passwordSetAt: number,\n  policy: PasswordPolicy = {}\n): number | null {\n  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n  const expirationDays = mergedPolicy.passwordExpirationDays ?? 0;\n  const gracePeriodDays = mergedPolicy.passwordGracePeriodDays ?? 0;\n\n  if (expirationDays === 0 || gracePeriodDays === 0) {\n    return null; // No expiration or grace period\n  }\n\n  const expirationMs = expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n  const gracePeriodMs = gracePeriodDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n  const now = Date.now();\n  const expiresAt = passwordSetAt + expirationMs;\n  const graceEndsAt = expiresAt + gracePeriodMs;\n\n  if (now <= expiresAt) {\n    return null; // Not expired yet\n  }\n\n  if (now > graceEndsAt) {\n    return 0; // Grace period ended\n  }\n\n  // Calculate remaining days in grace period\n  const remainingMs = graceEndsAt - now;\n  return Math.ceil(remainingMs / (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000));\n}\n\n/**\n * Get days until password expiration.\n *\n * @param passwordSetAt - Timestamp when password was set\n * @param policy - Optional custom policy\n * @returns Days until expiration, or null if no expiration\n */\nexport function getDaysUntilExpiration(\n  passwordSetAt: number,\n  policy: PasswordPolicy = {}\n): number | null {\n  const mergedPolicy = { ...DEFAULT_PASSWORD_POLICY, ...policy };\n  const expirationDays = mergedPolicy.passwordExpirationDays ?? 0;\n\n  if (expirationDays === 0) {\n    return null; // No expiration\n  }\n\n  const expirationMs = passwordSetAt + expirationDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n  const remainingMs = expirationMs - Date.now();\n\n  if (remainingMs <= 0) {\n    return 0; // Already expired\n  }\n\n  return Math.ceil(remainingMs / (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000));\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a warningDays;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Utilities\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Generate a secure random password.\n *\n * @param length - Password length (default: 16)\n * @param options - Options for password generation\n * @returns Generated password\n */\nexport function generateSecurePassword(\n  length = 16,\n  options: {\n    includeUppercase?: boolean;\n    includeLowercase?: boolean;\n    includeNumbers?: boolean;\n    includeSpecialChars?: boolean;\n    excludeAmbiguous?: boolean; // Exclude 0OIl1\n  } = {}\n): string {\n  const {\n    includeUppercase = true,\n    includeLowercase = true,\n    includeNumbers = true,\n    includeSpecialChars = true,\n    excludeAmbiguous = false,\n  } = options;\n\n  const uppercase = \"ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ\" + (excludeAmbiguous ? \"\" : \"IO\");\n  const lowercase = \"abcdefghjkmnpqrstuvwxyz\" + (excludeAmbiguous ? \"\" : \"ilo\");\n  const numbers = \"23456789\" + (excludeAmbiguous ? \"\" : \"01\");\n  const special = \"!@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;:,.<>?\";\n\n  let charset = \"\";\n  if (includeLowercase) charset += lowercase;\n  if (includeUppercase) charset += uppercase;\n  if (includeNumbers) charset += numbers;\n  if (includeSpecialChars) charset += special;\n\n  if (charset.length === 0) {\n    charset = lowercase + numbers;\n  }\n\n  // Ensure at least one character from each selected type is included\n  let password = \"\";\n  const guaranteed: string[] = [];\n  if (includeLowercase) {\n    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n    guaranteed.push(lowercase[array[0]! % lowercase.length]!);\n  }\n  if (includeUppercase) {\n    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n    guaranteed.push(uppercase[array[0]! % uppercase.length]!);\n  }\n  if (includeNumbers) {\n    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n    guaranteed.push(numbers[array[0]! % numbers.length]!);\n  }\n  if (includeSpecialChars) {\n    const array = new Uint8Array(1);\n    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n    guaranteed.push(special[array[0]! % special.length]!);\n  }\n\n  // Fill the rest randomly\n  const remainingLength = length - guaranteed.length;\n  if (remainingLength > 0) {\n    const array = new Uint8Array(remainingLength);\n    crypto.getRandomValues(array);\n    for (let i = 0; i < remainingLength; i++) {\n      password += charset[array[i]! % charset.length];\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Add guaranteed characters and shuffle\n  password += guaranteed.join(\"\");\n  const passwordArray = password.split(\"\");\n  for (let i = passwordArray.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {\n    const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1));\n    [passwordArray[i], passwordArray[j]] = [passwordArray[j]!, passwordArray[i]!];\n  }\n\n  return passwordArray.join(\"\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Get password policy */\n102\t  score: number;\n103\t  /** Event details */\n104\t  details?: Record<string, unknown>;\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\t/**\n108\t * Session risk assessment.\n109\t */\n110\texport interface SessionRiskAssessment {\n111\t  /** Overall risk score (0-100) */\n112\t  riskScore: number;\n113\t  /** Risk level */\n114\t  riskLevel: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n115\t  /** Risk factors identified */\n116\t  riskFactors: string[];\n117\t  /** Recommended action */\n118\t  recommendedAction: \"allow\" | \"monitor\" | \"challenge\" | \"block\";\n119\t  /** Security events that contributed to the score */\n120\t  events: SecurityEvent[];\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t/**\n124\t * Device trust level.\n125\t */\n126\texport type DeviceTrustLevel = \"unknown\" | \"untrusted\" | \"trusted\" | \"highly_trusted\";\n127\t\n128\t/**\n129\t * Device trust information.\n130\t */\n131\texport interface DeviceTrustInfo {\n132\t  /** Device ID */\n133\t  deviceId: string;\n134\t  /** Trust level */\n135\t  trustLevel: DeviceTrustLevel;\n136\t  /** First seen timestamp */\n137\t  firstSeenAt: number;\n138\t  /** Last seen timestamp */\n139\t  lastSeenAt: number;\n140\t  /** Number of successful authentications */\n141\t  successfulAuths: number;\n142\t  /** Last risk assessment */\n143\t  lastRiskAssessment?: SessionRiskAssessment;\n144\t}\n145\t\n146\t// ============================================================================\n147\t// IP Address Utilities\n148\t// ============================================================================\n149\t\n150\t/**\n151\t * Parse an IP address into its components.\n152\t */\n153\texport function parseIpAddress(ip: string): IpInfo {\n154\t  if (ip.includes(\".\")) {\n155\t    // IPv4\n156\t    const octets = ip.split(\".\").map((n) => parseInt(n, 10));\n157\t\n158\t    return {\n159\t      ip,\n160\t      type: \"ipv4\",\n161\t      octets,\n162\t      isPrivate: isPrivateIpv4(octets),\n163\t      isLoopback: octets[0] === 127,\n164\t    };\n165\t  }\n166\t\n167\t  if (ip.includes(\":\")) {\n168\t    // IPv6\n169\t    const hextets = ip.split(\":\");\n170\t\n171\t    return {\n172\t      ip,\n173\t      type: \"ipv6\",\n174\t      hextets,\n175\t      isPrivate: ip.startsWith(\"fc\") || ip.startsWith(\"fd\") || ip.startsWith(\"fe80\"),\n176\t      isLoopback: ip === \"::1\",\n177\t    };\n178\t  }\n179\t\n180\t  return {\n181\t    ip,\n182\t    type: \"unknown\",\n183\t    isPrivate: false,\n184\t    isLoopback: false,\n185\t  };\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/**\n189\t * Check if IPv4 address is private.\n190\t */\n191\tfunction isPrivateIpv4(octets: number[]): boolean {\n192\t  const [first, second] = octets;\n193\t\n194\t  // 10.0.0.0/8\n195\t  if (first === 10) return true;\n196\t\n197\t  // 172.16.0.0/12\n198\t  if (first === 172 && second >= 16 && second <= 31) return true;\n199\t\n200\t  // 192.168.0.0/16\n201\t  if (first === 192 && second === 168) return true;\n202\t\n203\t  // 169.254.0.0/16 (link-local)\n204\t  if (first === 169 && second === 254) return true;\n205\t\n206\t  return false;\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\t/**\n210\t * Check if two IP addresses are in the same subnet.\n211\t *\n212\t * For IPv4, checks the specified number of leading octets.\n213\t * For IPv6, checks the specified number of leading hextets.\n214\t */\n215\texport function areIpsInSameSubnet(ip1: string, ip2: string, prefixLength = 24): boolean {\n216\t  const info1 = parseIpAddress(ip1);\n217\t  const info2 = parseIpAddress(ip2);\n218\t\n219\t  if (info1.type !== info2.type || info1.type === \"unknown\") {\n220\t    return false;\n221\t  }\n222\t\n223\t  if (info1.type === \"ipv4\" && info1.octets && info2.octets) {\n224\t    // For IPv4, prefixLength / 8 gives number of octets to compare\n225\t    const octetsToCompare = Math.floor(prefixLength / 8);\n226\t\n227\t    for (let i = 0; i < octetsToCompare && i < 4; i++) {\n228\t      if (info1.octets[i] !== info2.octets[i]) {\n229\t        return false;\n230\t      }\n231\t    }\n232\t\n233\t    // Check remaining bits\n234\t    const remainingBits = prefixLength % 8;\n235\t    if (remainingBits > 0 && octetsToCompare < 4) {\n236\t      const mask = 256 - Math.pow(2, 8 - remainingBits);\n237\t      if ((info1.octets[octetsToCompare]! & mask) !== (info2.octets[octetsToCompare]! & mask)) {\n238\t        return false;\n239\t      }\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    return true;\n243\t  }\n244\t\n245\t  if (info1.type === \"ipv6\" && info1.hextets && info2.hextets) {\n246\t    // For IPv6, prefixLength / 16 gives number of hextets to compare\n247\t    const hextetsToCompare = Math.floor(prefixLength / 16);\n248\t\n249\t    for (let i = 0; i < hextetsToCompare && i < 8; i++) {\n250\t      if (info1.hextets[i] !== info2.hextets[i]) {\n251\t        return false;\n252\t      }\n253\t    }\n254\t\n255\t    return true;\n256\t  }\n257\t\n258\t  return false;\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t/**\n262\t * Calculate the distance between two IP addresses.\n263\t *\n264\t * Returns a normalized \"distance\" value (0-100) based on how different the IPs are.\n265\t * Useful for detecting proxy/VPN changes vs. location changes.\n266\t */\n267\texport function calculateIpDistance(ip1: string, ip2: string): number {\n268\t  const info1 = parseIpAddress(ip1);\n269\t  const info2 = parseIpAddress(ip2);\n270\t\n271\t  if (info1.type !== info2.type || info1.type === \"unknown\") {\n272\t    return 100; // Maximum distance for different IP types\n273\t  }\n274\t\n275\t  if (info1.type === \"ipv4\" && info1.octets && info2.octets) {\n276\t    // Calculate difference for each octet\n277\t    let totalDiff = 0;\n278\t    for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n279\t      totalDiff += Math.abs(info1.octets[i]! - info2.octets[i]!);\n280\t    }\n281\t\n282\t    // Normalize to 0-100\n283\t    return Math.min(100, Math.floor(totalDiff / 2.55));\n284\t  }\n285\t\n286\t  return 50; // Middle distance for IPv6 (simplified)\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\t/**\n290\t * Get a simplified IP class for grouping.\n291\t *\n292\t * Returns \"A\", \"B\", \"C\" class for IPv4, useful for coarse-grained comparison.\n293\t */\n294\texport function getIpClass(ip: string): string {\n295\t  const info = parseIpAddress(ip);\n296\t\n297\t  if (info.type === \"ipv4\" && info.octets) {\n298\t    const [first] = info.octets;\n299\t\n300\t    if (first < 128) return \"A\";\n301\t    if (first < 192) return \"B\";\n302\t    return \"C\";\n303\t  }\n304\t\n305\t  return \"unknown\";\n306\t}\n307\t\n308\t// ============================================================================\n309\t// User Agent Analysis\n310\t// ============================================================================\n311\t\n312\t/**\n313\t * Analyze a user agent string.\n314\t */\n315\texport function analyzeUserAgent(userAgent: string): UserAgentInfo {\n316\t  const ua = userAgent.toLowerCase();\n317\t  const info: UserAgentInfo = {\n318\t    raw: userAgent,\n319\t    deviceType: \"unknown\",\n320\t  };\n321\t\n322\t  // Detect browser\n323\t  if (ua.includes(\"edg/\") || ua.includes(\"edge/\")) {\n324\t    info.browser = \"edge\";\n325\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"opr/\") || ua.includes(\"opera/\")) {\n326\t    info.browser = \"opera\";\n327\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"brave/\")) {\n328\t    info.browser = \"brave\";\n329\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"chrome/\") && !ua.includes(\"edg\")) {\n330\t    info.browser = \"chrome\";\n331\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"safari/\") && !ua.includes(\"chrome\")) {\n332\t    info.browser = \"safari\";\n333\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"firefox/\")) {\n334\t    info.browser = \"firefox\";\n335\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"trident/\") || ua.includes(\"msie\")) {\n336\t    info.browser = \"ie\";\n337\t  }\n338\t\n339\t  // Detect OS\n340\t  if (ua.includes(\"windows\")) {\n341\t    info.os = \"windows\";\n342\t    if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 10.0\")) info.osVersion = \"10\";\n343\t    else if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 6.3\")) info.osVersion = \"8.1\";\n344\t    else if (ua.includes(\"windows nt 6.1\")) info.osVersion = \"7\";\n345\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"iphone\") || ua.includes(\"ipad\") || ua.includes(\"ipod\")) {\n346\t    info.os = \"ios\";\n347\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"android\")) {\n348\t    info.os = \"android\";\n349\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"mac os x\") || ua.includes(\"macos\")) {\n350\t    info.os = \"macos\";\n351\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"linux\")) {\n352\t    info.os = \"linux\";\n353\t  }\n354\t\n355\t  // Detect device type\n356\t  if (/bot|crawl|spider|slurp|curl|wget/i.test(userAgent)) {\n357\t    info.deviceType = \"bot\";\n358\t  } else if (/tablet|ipad|playbook|silk/i.test(ua)) {\n359\t    info.deviceType = \"tablet\";\n360\t  } else if (/mobile|android|iphone|ipod|blackberry|iemobile|opera mini/i.test(ua)) {\n361\t    info.deviceType = \"mobile\";\n362\t  } else if (info.os) {\n363\t    info.deviceType = \"desktop\";\n364\t  }\n365\t\n366\t  // Detect hardware\n367\t  if (ua.includes(\"arm\")) {\n368\t    info.hardware = \"arm\";\n369\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"x86_64\") || ua.includes(\"x64\") || ua.includes(\"wow64\")) {\n370\t    info.hardware = \"x64\";\n371\t  } else if (ua.includes(\"i686\") || ua.includes(\"x86\")) {\n372\t    info.hardware = \"x86\";\n373\t  }\n374\t\n375\t  return info;\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t/**\n379\t * Calculate similarity between two user agent strings.\n380\t *\n381\t * Returns 0-100 similarity score.\n382\t */\n383\texport function calculateUserAgentSimilarity(ua1: string, ua2: string): number {\n384\t  const info1 = analyzeUserAgent(ua1);\n385\t  const info2 = analyzeUserAgent(ua2);\n386\t\n387\t  let similarity = 0;\n388\t  let factors = 0;\n389\t\n390\t  // Browser match\n391\t  if (info1.browser === info2.browser) {\n392\t    similarity += 30;\n393\t  }\n394\t  factors++;\n395\t\n396\t  // OS match\n397\t  if (info1.os === info2.os) {\n398\t    similarity += 30;\n399\t  }\n400\t  factors++;\n401\t\n402\t  // Device type match\n403\t  if (info1.deviceType === info2.deviceType) {\n404\t    similarity += 20;\n405\t  }\n406\t  factors++;\n407\t\n408\t  // Hardware match\n409\t  if (info1.hardware === info2.hardware) {\n410\t    similarity += 20;\n411\t  }\n412\t  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n413\t  factors++;\n414\t\n415\t  return Math.min(100, similarity);\n416\t}\n417\t\n418\t/**\n419\t * Detect if user agent change is legitimate.\n420\t *\n421\t * Returns true if the change is likely a browser update or minor version change.\n422\t */\n423\texport function isLegitimateUserAgentChange(oldUa: string, newUa: string): boolean {\n424\t  const oldInfo = analyzeUserAgent(oldUa);\n425\t  const newInfo = analyzeUserAgent(newUa);\n426\t\n427\t  // Same browser and OS is likely a version update\n428\t  if (oldInfo.browser === newInfo.browser && oldInfo.os === newInfo.os) {\n429\t    return true;\n430\t  }\n431\t\n432\t  // Same device type with different browser could be user trying new browser\n433\t  if (oldInfo.deviceType === newInfo.deviceType && oldInfo.os === newInfo.os) {\n434\t    return true;\n435\t  }\n436\t\n437\t  return false;\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t// ============================================================================\n441\t// Session Risk Assessment\n442\t// ============================================================================\n443\t\n444\t/**\n445\t * Security event tracking for session risk scoring.\n446\t */\n447\tconst sessionSecurityEvents = new Map<string, SecurityEvent[]>();\n448\t\n449\t/**\n450\t * Assess session security risk.\n451\t *\n452\t * Analyzes a session and current request to calculate a risk score.\n453\t */\n454\texport async function assessSessionRisk(\n455\t  session: AuthSession,\n456\t  currentIp: string,\n457\t  currentUserAgent?: string,\n458\t  options: {\n459\t    /** Time window for event history (ms) */\n460\t    eventHistoryWindow?: number;\n461\t    /** Whether to include geolocation checks */\n462\t    includeGeolocation?: boolean;\n463\t    /** Known geolocation data (optional) */\n464\t    knownGeo?: (ip: string) => Promise<GeolocationData | null>;\n465\t  } = {}\n466\t): Promise<SessionRiskAssessment> {\n467\t  const {\n468\t    eventHistoryWindow = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 24 hours\n469\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n470\t    includeGeolocation = false,\n471\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n472\t    knownGeo,\n473\t  } = options;\n474\t\n475\t  const riskFactors: string[] = [];\n476\t  const events: SecurityEvent[] = [];\n477\t  let riskScore = 0;\n478\t\n479\t  const now = Date.now();\n480\t\n481\t  // Get recent security events for this session\n482\t  const recentEvents = (sessionSecurityEvents.get(session.sessionId) || []).filter(\n483\t    (e) => now - e.timestamp < eventHistoryWindow\n484\t  );\n485\t\n486\t  // Check IP change\n487\t  if (session.clientIp !== currentIp) {\n488\t    const ipInfo = parseIpAddress(currentIp);\n489\t    const oldIpInfo = parseIpAddress(session.clientIp);\n490\t\n491\t    if (ipInfo.type !== oldIpInfo.type) {\n492\t      riskScore += 40;\n493\t      riskFactors.push(\"IP address type changed (IPv4 ↔ IPv6)\");\n494\t      events.push({\n495\t        type: \"ip_type_change\",\n496\t        timestamp: now,\n497\t        score: 40,\n498\t        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n499\t      });\n500\t    } else if (!areIpsInSameSubnet(session.clientIp, currentIp, 24)) {\n501\t      riskScore += 30;\n502\t      riskFactors.push(\"IP address changed to different subnet\");\n503\t      events.push({\n504\t        type: \"ip_subnet_change\",\n505\t        timestamp: now,\n506\t        score: 30,\n507\t        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n508\t      });\n509\t    } else {\n510\t      riskScore += 10;\n511\t      riskFactors.push(\"IP address changed within same subnet\");\n512\t      events.push({\n513\t        type: \"ip_change_same_subnet\",\n514\t        timestamp: now,\n515\t        score: 10,\n516\t        details: { oldIp: session.clientIp, newIp: currentIp },\n517\t      });\n518\t    }\n519\t  }\n520\t\n521\t  // Check User-Agent change\n522\t  if (currentUserAgent && session.userAgent && session.userAgent !== currentUserAgent) {\n523\t    const similarity = calculateUserAgentSimilarity(session.userAgent, currentUserAgent);\n524\t\n525\t    if (similarity < 50) {\n526\t      riskScore += 30;\n527\t      riskFactors.push(\"User-Agent changed significantly\");\n528\t      events.push({\n529\t        type: \"ua_change_major\",\n530\t        timestamp: now,\n531\t        score: 30,\n532\t        details: { oldUa: session.userAgent, newUa: currentUserAgent, similarity },\n533\t      });\n534\t    } else if (!isLegitimateUserAgentChange(session.userAgent, currentUserAgent)) {\n535\t      riskScore += 15;\n536\t      riskFactors.push(\"User-Agent changed (possibly suspicious)\");\n537\t      events.push({\n538\t        type: \"ua_change_minor\",\n539\t        timestamp: now,\n540\t        score: 15,\n541\t        details: { oldUa: session.userAgent, newUa: currentUserAgent },\n542\t      });\n543\t    }\n544\t  }\n545\t\n546\t  // Check session age (very new sessions are higher risk)\n547\t  const sessionAge = now - session.createdAt;\n548\t  if (sessionAge < 5 * 60 * 1000) {\n549\t    // Less than 5 minutes old\n550\t    riskScore += 5;\n551\t    riskFactors.push(\"Very new session\");\n552\t  }\n553\t\n554\t  // Check idle time (long idle may indicate hijacking)\n555\t  const idleTime = now - session.lastActivityAt;\n556\t  if (idleTime > 60 * 60 * 1000) {\n557\t    // More than 1 hour idle\n558\t    riskScore += 10;\n559\t    riskFactors.push(\"Long idle time before activity\");\n560\t  }\n561\t\n562\t  // Include recent security events\n563\t  for (const event of recentEvents) {\n564\t    riskScore += event.score;\n565\t    events.push(event);\n566\t  }\n567\t\n568\t  // Cap risk score at 100\n569\t  riskScore = Math.min(100, riskScore);\n570\t\n571\t  // Determine risk level\n572\t  let riskLevel: \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"critical\";\n573\t  let recommendedAction: \"allow\" | \"monitor\" | \"challenge\" | \"block\";\n574\t\n575\t  if (riskScore < 20) {\n576\t    riskLevel = \"low\";\n577\t    recommendedAction = \"allow\";\n578\t  } else if (riskScore < 50) {\n579\t    riskLevel = \"medium\";\n580\t    recommendedAction = \"monitor\";\n581\t  } else if (riskScore < 80) {\n582\t    riskLevel = \"high\";\n583\t    recommendedAction = \"challenge\";\n584\t  } else {\n585\t    riskLevel = \"critical\";\n586\t    recommendedAction = \"block\";\n587\t  }\n588\t\n589\t  return {\n590\t    riskScore,\n591\t    riskLevel,\n592\t    riskFactors,\n593\t    recommendedAction,\n594\t    events,\n595\t  };\n596\t}\n597\t\n598\t/**\n599\t * Record a security event for a session.\n600\t */\n601\texport function recordSecurityEvent(sessionId: string, event: SecurityEvent): void {\n602\t  const events = sessionSecurityEvents.get(sessionId) || [];\n603\t  events.push(event);\n604\t\n605\t  // Keep only last 100 events per session\n606\t  if (events.length > 100) {\n607\t    events.shift();\n608\t  }\n609\t\n610\t  sessionSecurityEvents.set(sessionId, events);\n611\t\n612\t  logger.debug(\"Security event recorded\", {\n613\t    sessionId,\n614\t    eventType: event.type,\n615\t    score: event.score,\n616\t  });\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t/**\n620\t * Clear security events for a session.\n621\t */\n622\texport function clearSecurityEvents(sessionId: string): void {\n623\t  sessionSecurityEvents.delete(sessionId);\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// ============================================================================\n627\t// Device Trust Management\n628\t// ============================================================================\n629\t\n630\t/**\n631\t * Device trust storage.\n632\t */\n633\tconst deviceTrustStorage = new Map<string, DeviceTrustInfo>();\n634\t\n635\t/**\n636\t * Get or create device trust info.\n637\t */\n638\texport function getOrCreateDeviceTrust(\n639\t  deviceId: string,\n640\t  initialRiskAssessment?: SessionRiskAssessment\n641\t): DeviceTrustInfo {\n642\t  const existing = deviceTrustStorage.get(deviceId);\n643\t\n644\t  if (existing) {\n645\t    return existing;\n646\t  }\n647\t\n648\t  const trustInfo: DeviceTrustInfo = {\n649\t    deviceId,\n650\t    trustLevel: \"unknown\",\n651\t    firstSeenAt: Date.now(),\n652\t    lastSeenAt: Date.now(),\n653\t    successfulAuths: 0,\n654\t    lastRiskAssessment: initialRiskAssessment,\n655\t  };\n656\t\n657\t  deviceTrustStorage.set(deviceId, trustInfo);\n658\t\n659\t  logger.info(\"New device trust record created\", { deviceId });\n660\t\n661\t  return trustInfo;\n662\t}\n663\t\n664\t/**\n665\t * Update device trust based on successful authentication.\n666\t */\n667\texport function updateDeviceTrust(deviceId: string, riskAssessment?: SessionRiskAssessment): void {\n668\t  const trustInfo = getOrCreateDeviceTrust(deviceId, riskAssessment);\n669\t\n670\t  // Ensure lastSeenAt is always at least 1ms after firstSeenAt\n671\t  const now = Date.now();\n672\t  trustInfo.lastSeenAt = Math.max(now, trustInfo.firstSeenAt + 1);\n673\t  trustInfo.successfulAuths++;\n674\t  trustInfo.lastRiskAssessment = riskAssessment;\n675\t\n676\t  // Auto-promote trust based on successful auths\n677\t  if (trustInfo.successfulAuths >= 10 && trustInfo.trustLevel !== \"highly_trusted\") {\n678\t    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"highly_trusted\";\n679\t    logger.info(\"Device promoted to highly trusted\", { deviceId });\n680\t  } else if (trustInfo.successfulAuths >= 5 && trustInfo.trustLevel === \"unknown\") {\n681\t    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"trusted\";\n682\t    logger.info(\"Device promoted to trusted\", { deviceId });\n683\t  } else if (riskAssessment && riskAssessment.riskLevel === \"high\") {\n684\t    trustInfo.trustLevel = \"untrusted\";\n685\t    logger.warn(\"Device marked as untrusted due to high risk\", { deviceId });\n686\t  }\n687\t\n688\t  deviceTrustStorage.set(deviceId, trustInfo);\n689\t}\n690\t\n691\t/**\n692\t * Check if a device is trusted.\n693\t */\n694\texport function isDeviceTrusted(deviceId: string): boolean {\n695\t  const trustInfo = deviceTrustStorage.get(deviceId);\n696\t  return trustInfo?.trustLevel === \"trusted\" || trustInfo?.trustLevel === \"highly_trusted\";\n697\t}\n698\t\n699\t/**\n700\t * Get device trust level.\n701\t */\n702\texport function getDeviceTrustLevel(deviceId: string): DeviceTrustLevel {\n703\t  return deviceTrustStorage.get(deviceId)?.trustLevel || \"unknown\";\n704\t}\n705\t\n706\t/**\n707\t * Manually set device trust level.\n708\t */\n709\texport function setDeviceTrustLevel(deviceId: string, trustLevel: DeviceTrustLevel): void {\n710\t  const trustInfo = getOrCreateDeviceTrust(deviceId);\n711\t  trustInfo.trustLevel = trustLevel;\n712\t  deviceTrustStorage.set(deviceId, trustInfo);\n713\t\n714\t  logger.info(\"Device trust level manually set\", { deviceId, trustLevel });\n715\t}\n716\t\n717\t/**\n718\t * Remove device trust record.\n719\t */\n720\texport function removeDeviceTrust(deviceId: string): void {\n721\t  deviceTrustStorage.delete(deviceId);\n722\t  logger.info(\"Device trust record removed\", { deviceId });\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// ============================================================================\n726\t// Impossible Travel Detection\n727\t// ============================================================================\n728\t\n729\t/**\n730\t * Maximum plausible travel speed (km/h).\n731\t * Commercial jets fly at ~900 km/h, so anything faster is suspicious.\n732\t */\n733\tconst MAX_PLAUSIBLE_SPEED_KMH = 900;\n734\t\n735\t/**\n736\t * Check for impossible travel between two locations.\n737\t *\n738\t * Compares the time between two events with the distance between\n739\t * their locations to detect impossible travel scenarios.\n740\t */\n741\texport async function detectImpossibleTravel(\n742\t  event1: { timestamp: number; ip: string; geo?: GeolocationData },\n743\t  event2: { timestamp: number; ip: string; geo?: GeolocationData },\n744\t  options: {\n745\t    /** Maximum plausible speed (km/h) */\n746\t    maxSpeedKmh?: number;\n747\t    /** Geolocation lookup function */\n748\t    geoLookup?: (ip: string) => Promise<GeolocationData | null>;\n749\t  } = {}\n750\t): Promise<{ impossible: boolean; speed?: number; distance?: number }> {\n751\t  const { maxSpeedKmh = MAX_PLAUSIBLE_SPEED_KMH, geoLookup } = options;\n752\t\n753\t  // Ensure event1 is earlier\n754\t  const [earlier, later] =\n755\t    event1.timestamp < event2.timestamp ? [event1, event2] : [event2, event1];\n756\t\n757\t  // Get geolocation data\n758\t  const geo1 = event1.geo || (geoLookup ? await geoLookup(event1.ip) : null);\n759\t  const geo2 = event2.geo || (geoLookup ? await geoLookup(event2.ip) : null);\n760\t\n761\t  if (!geo1 || !geo2 || !geo1.latitude || !geo1.longitude || !geo2.latitude || !geo2.longitude) {\n762\t    // Can't determine without geolocation\n763\t    return { impossible: false };\n764\t  }\n765\t\n766\t  // Calculate distance using Haversine formula\n767\t  const distance = calculateDistance(geo1.latitude, geo1.longitude, geo2.latitude, geo2.longitude);\n768\t\n769\t  // Calculate time difference in hours\n770\t  const timeDiffHours = (later.timestamp - earlier.timestamp) / (1000 * 60 * 60);\n771\t\n772\t  if (timeDiffHours <= 0) {\n773\t    // Events at the same time or reversed timestamps\n774\t    return { impossible: false };\n775\t  }\n776\t\n777\t  // Calculate required speed\n778\t  const speed = distance / timeDiffHours;\n779\t\n780\t  if (speed > maxSpeedKmh) {\n781\t    return { impossible: true, speed, distance };\n782\t  }\n783\t\n784\t  return { impossible: false, speed, distance };\n785\t}\n786\t\n787\t/**\n788\t * Calculate distance between two coordinates using the Haversine formula.\n789\t *\n790\t * Returns distance in kilometers.\n791\t */\n792\texport function calculateDistance(lat1: number, lon1: number, lat2: number, lon2: number): number {\n793\t  const R = 6371; // Earth's radius in km\n794\t  const dLat = toRadians(lat2 - lat1);\n795\t  const dLon = toRadians(lon2 - lon1);\n796\t\n797\t  const a =\n798\t    Math.sin(dLat / 2) * Math.sin(dLat / 2) +\n799\t    Math.cos(toRadians(lat1)) * Math.cos(toRadians(lat2)) * Math.sin(dLon / 2) * Math.sin(dLon / 2);\n800\t\n801\t  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a));\n802\t\n803\t  return R * c;\n804\t}\n805\t\n806\t/**\n807\t * Convert degrees to radians.\n808\t */\n809\tfunction toRadians(degrees: number): number {\n810\t  return degrees * (Math.PI / 180);\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\t// ============================================================================\n814\t// Middleware Integration\n815\t// ============================================================================\n816\t\n817\t/**\n818\t * Session security middleware options.\n819\t */\n820\texport interface boolean;\n825\t  /** Risk threshold for blocking (0-100) */\n826\t  riskThreshold?: number;\n827\t  /** Whether to require re-authentication on high risk */\n828\t  reauthOnHighRisk?: boolean;\n829\t}\n830\t\n831\t/**\n832\t * Session security middleware.\n833\t *\n834\t * Enhances session validation with risk assessment and additional security checks.\n835\t */\n836\texport function sessionSecurity(options: SessionSecurityMiddlewareOptions = {}) {\n837\t  const {\n838\t    enforceIpBinding = true,\n839\t    checkUserAgent = true,\n840\t    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n841\t    riskThreshold = 80,\n842\t    reauthOnHighRisk = true,\n843\t  } = options;\n844\t\n845\t  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any\n846\t  return async (c: any, next: any) => {\n847\t    const session = c.get(\"session\");\n848\t    if (!session) {\n849\t      return next();\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    const clientIp =\n853\t      c.req.header(\"CF-Connecting-IP\") ||\n854\t      c.req.header(\"X-Forwarded-For\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() ||\n855\t      c.req.header(\"X-Real-IP\") ||\n856\t      \"unknown\";\n857\t\n858\t    const userAgent = c.req.header(\"User-Agent\");\n859\t\n860\t    // Enforce IP binding if enabled and session has ipBinding set\n861\t    if (enforceIpBinding && session.ipBinding && session.clientIp) {\n862\t      // Check if IP is in the same /24 subnet (allows for DHCP changes within same network)\n863\t      const ipInfo = parseIpAddress(clientIp);\n864\t      const sessionIpInfo = parseIpAddress(session.clientIp);\n865\t\n866\t      if (ipInfo.type === sessionIpInfo.type && ipInfo.type === \"ipv4\") {\n867\t        // {\n871\t          securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n872\t            c,\n873\t            \"ip_binding_violation\",\n874\t            `Session IP binding violation: ${session.clientIp} -> ${clientIp}`\n875\t          );\n876\t          throw new Error(\"Session IP binding violation - session terminated\");\n877\t        }\n878\t      } else if (ipInfo.type === sessionIpInfo.type && ipInfo.type === \"ipv6\") {\n879\t        // For IPv6, check if within same /64 subnet\n880\t        const ipsInSameSubnet = areIpsInSameSubnet(session.clientIp, clientIp, 64);\n881\t\n882\t        if ${session.clientIp} -> ${clientIp}`\n887\t          );\n888\t          throw new Error(\"Session IP binding violation - session terminated\");\n889\t        }\n890\t      } else if (ipInfo.type !== sessionIpInfo.type) {\n891\t        // IP type changed (IPv4 <-> IPv6)\n892\t        securityLogger.logSuspiciousActivity(\n893\t          c,\n894\t          \"ip_type_change\",\n895\t          `Session IP type change: ${sessionIpInfo.type} -> ${ipInfo.type}`\n896\t        );\n897\t        throw new Error(\"Session IP type changed - session terminated\");\n898\t      }\n899\t    }\n900\t\n901\t    // Check User-Agent changes if enabled\n902\t    if (checkUserAgent && session.userAgent && userAgent !== session.userAgent) {\n903\t      const similarity = calculateUserAgentSimilarity(session.userAgent, userAgent);\n904\t\n905\t      // Significant user agent change without being a legitimate update\n906\t      if (similarity < 50 &&
